text: Horrible customer service, discrimination, and profiling.  First let me start by saying no one greeted me as I entered the store. I walked around a good 10 minutes before anyone asked if I need assistance. Which seemed odd because, an Asian man (who I perceive to be the manager/owner) was helping another woman next to me. After showing her several watches, he recommended one I was particularly interested in. The woman glanced over the watch for an extended period of time, tried it on then gave it back to him. Before he put it back in the case, I politely, asked if I could see the watch next. The Asian man informed me that I can look at it, but was not at liberty to touch it. Mind you this is after I watched another woman casually handle the watch for as long as she wanted. In total disbelief, I turned and walk upstairs where my daughter is looking at other watches. The Asian man then yells upstairs for another assistant to help me. I tell my daughter that we will no longer be purchasing anything from here and proceed to walk out the door. text: Worst customer service. I bought two watches on Jan 7, 2023. The male associate assisted me at first, he sold me two watches. Before buying, I asked him what the return policy is, he confirmed I can return within 30 days. After finalized my payment, I decided to exchange one of the two watches for a nicer one. A different associate (the manager) assisted me with this exchange, I checked with the associate again about the return policy. She said "No, exchange for same or higher value only." I told her the other associate literally told me 5 minutes ago, he said it was returnable. The manager did not ask any question, or even apologetic, she just said "No, it's exchange only!", then she went inside the employee room without saying anything and left me with a different associate. I exchanged for a nicer watch anyway, because I did want the nicer watch. After I finalized the exchange, the associate told me, "Oh, you can only exchange once!" I was stunned again. The associate then said, "Oh I thought we already told you!" I was really upset. The manager then came out, following was the guy who sold me the watches at the beginning, none of them was apologetic. I asked the guy why he told me many times, I could return, but then the manager said no. He said, "well I didn't know the policy changed, I just started here!" No apology, no further explanation from any of them. They just shrugged it off, no customer service whatsoever after the purchase finalized.  Movado make good watches, but this poor customer service, the kind of service that only cares about bonuses and commissions bring the value of Movado down. Shameful and sleazy! If you really want to buy a Movado watch, do not buy it at the Movado store in the Potomac Mills, these associates do not deserve any commissions and bonuses from your purchase.
address: 2700 Potomac Mills Cir Suite 940, Woodbridge, VA 22191

text: This is a compliment for Kim at the Potomac Mills location.  My Movado watch that I purchased a few years back from this location had kinks in the links that I could not get out. I took it to the store to see if they could do anything to fix it. Kim and the staff worked on fixing my watch but it was not entirely working. They all worked really hard on it and the staff was friendly while I waited and showed me watches while I waited. When they determined that it was not entirely fixable, Kim shared some options with me and showed genuine concern for my dilemma with the watch, which is my favorite watch! I purchased two matching watches for my significant other and I as a gift a few years ago.  Kim offered to swap out my watch for the same watch and that was an option I was not expecting. This was a great option for me because the other watch I had purchased was not having the same issue I was experiencing, so I went with it.  I'm not sure if Kim is the manager of the store or not, but either way, Kim provided excellent customer service and represented the brand and team very well. text: Exceptional Customer Service & Unforgettable Experience!  I had the absolute pleasure of shopping at the Movado Company Store at Tanger Outlets in Oxon Hill, MD, and I couldn’t have asked for a better experience! From the moment I walked in, I was warmly greeted by Christele and Raymond, who were both incredibly personable and genuinely excited to welcome me.  This was my first time purchasing a Movado watch, and they made it such a memorable occasion. The store itself is stunning—filled with a vast selection of beautiful, elegant timepieces, more than I’ve seen in any other local store. The atmosphere is sophisticated yet calming, with a “Quiet Storm” vibe that makes shopping feel luxurious and unrushed.  Christele took the time to walk me through the store’s exclusive sales and standout styles, ensuring I found something truly unique. Raymond was fantastic in helping me pick out the perfect watch for my dad, making the experience even more special. To top it all off, my birthday watch was elegantly wrapped with a bow, making the moment feel extra celebratory!  I left the store not just with a gorgeous new timepiece but with excitement for building my future Movado collection. Thank you, Christele and Raymond, for making my experience first-class and unforgettable! If you’re in the area and looking for a watch, this is the place to go!  #Movado #LuxuryExperience #TangerOutlets #FiveStarService
address: 6800 Oxon Hill Rd Suite 832, Oxon Hill, MD 20745

text: I visited the Movado store in Elizabeth on Cyber Monday after calling earlier in the morning to confirm pricing on two watches. The female representative I spoke with gave me a clear quote over the phone, so I drove to the store expecting the same information to be honored.  However, when I arrived, the male associate who helped me told me the watch was full price and only offered 20% off — even though the store was advertising 40–70% off throughout the sale. When I explained that I had called and been quoted a different price, he dismissed it and said that “unfortunately that was not the price.” His tone was rude, unprofessional, and the entire interaction felt very shady.  To make matters worse, every watch I showed interest in conveniently “wasn’t part of the sale,” which made the pricing feel inconsistent and misleading. It honestly felt like the staff may be working on commission because the behavior did not seem transparent at all.  The female representative later admitted she had given me the wrong quote, yet the store still refused to honor the price I was told. I was promised a call back from the manager the next day. I never received one.  Overall, this was a very frustrating and disappointing experience. I expected better from a brand like Movado.

text: I went in looking to perform a simple task which is just replacing a watch battery for a Movado watch . I have done this at other Movado stores with no issues . Upon entering i was greeted and asked what I needed , I said just a battery replacement . The fear and dread in both of the associates eyes was apparent . I asked how old is the watch ? One lady said we can’t change a watch battery if it’s over 10 years old. I’m like I don’t know the age let’s say it’s 1 day old. So she said  let me look at the watch , she then said she can’t change the battery because the watch has diamonds on it. I thought they were joking but she reaffirmed that they cannot change watch batteries with someone with diamonds on it although it’s a Movado watch with diamonds. So I confirmed you are not able to change a watch battery for a Movado watch at this Movado store? She was like well yes we can but then said no we we have only been open two months and not trained on it. Based on the posts it seems they can’t adjust wrist bands or change batteries which should be basic training for a factory watch store .

text: I had two polar opposite experiences in this store in the same day on 8/17/22.  The first visit earlier in the day was horrible and could have put me off as a long time customer for good if I didn't have such good experiences previously.  The second visit of the day was extremely positive so there is a happy ending!  I went in with the intent of either picking up an Ebel or a Concorde watch as a fan of both higher end lines already owning quite a few pieces.  I ended up being helped by a younger guy who after showing me two Ebel watches suggested condescendingly showing me "something more in MY price range like a Movado" well...he would not know my price range as he hadn't bothered to ask and I had not told him. I was dressed down and felt like it was flat out rude and I was upset after that so I just left the store. One should never make assumptions based on how someone's is dressed at least keep it to yourself sir you lost a sale and insulted a long time customer.  Now on to the positive! I went back later in the same day because I have made the trip out to this store and I was not leaving without what I wanted. Thankfully two lovely ladies had provided excellent service as my usual go to sales person I have worked with many times was not in that day. They sold me the watch I wanted while being so nice and pleasent. I was walked through all the features and details of the watch. They did not make assumptions and where glad to help me with my needs as a customer! This was a five star experience!
address: 807 Tanger Outlet Drive, Riverhead, NY 11901

text: This store has the worst customer service and the manager provided us false information. I bought a watch for my husband from Macys for his birthday. It stopped working after 10 months. He went to Macys to get it fixed but they told us it has to be Movado. We had the Movado warranty for 2 years so my husband called Movado and they told us to take it to a Movado store. When we took it to the store, the manager was like because we bought it from Macys they can’t do anything about it. Macys has to fix it. The manager was like if we want to replace the battery it will be $45. We then took it to the original Macys we bought it from since we didn’t wanna pay without knowing if it is a mechanical issue or not. Macys told us that Movado is the maker so original warranty is with them. My husband and I collectively wasted hours trying to get this watch fixed that we paid so much for. Terrible experience. I got 3 Movado watches so far. Well never buying again!!!!!
